query: I need to write the unit spec for the PlaceOrder Decider in my Cart service. The entity is CartEntity, the command is PlaceOrder, and the events are OrderPlaced and CartEmptied. Can you generate the RED Hspec file at tests/Decider/PlaceOrderSpec.hs using Decider.runDecision and Auth.emptyContext? I have three cases: cart not found → reject, cart already checked out → reject, valid cart → accept.

Write a projection spec for the StockSummary query that combines a StockItem entity. Test two cases: the empty case where no existing query exists and combine returns Update with zero reserved quantity, and the update case where an existing summary is replaced. Put it under tests/Projection/StockSummarySpec.hs with one assertion per it block.

add tests for the checkout command before i start implementing — i have the domain types ready in Stock.Core but no decide function yet, so the spec will be RED from a compile error first. just generate the skeleton with the three standard cases

I'm at step 3 of the outside-in TDD cycle for the Library feature. Domain modeling is done and the event model is verified. Now I need the failing unit specs for BorrowBook.decide and BookAvailability.combine. Please write both RED specs — one under tests/Decider/ and one under tests/Projection/ — before I move on to implement-command.

write an hspec spec for the outbound handler NotifyWarehouseOnStockReserved — it should verify that handleEvent emits 0 actions for non-matching events like StockReleased, and exactly 1 action for the matching StockReserved event. Use Integration.getActions to count the actions and pipe into shouldBe.

can u write a spec for the CreateCart decider? its a creation comand so entity should be Nothing when cart doesnt exist yet and it should call Decider.acceptNew. also need a case where it rejects if a cart already exists (entity is Just cart). put it in tests/Decider/CreateCartSpec.hs

The RemoveItem command needs unit tests before I implement it. It lives in src/Library/Commands/RemoveItem.hs, entity is LibraryCart, events are ItemRemoved. Write the Decider spec with three cases: cart not found → reject, item not in cart → reject, valid remove with existing item → accept. One it block per case, one shouldBe assertion each.

I want a unit test that documents our business invariant: the UpdateQuantity decide function must reject any command where the requested quantity would exceed the 99-item cap. Write the minimal Hspec assertion using Decider.runDecision and shouldBe (RejectCommand "Quantity exceeds maximum allowed"). The entity is StockItem and the command record has fields itemId and newQuantity.

Implement the decide function for the AddToCart command. The entity is CartEntity, the events are ItemAdded and CartCreated. It should reject when quantity is zero or negative, reject when the cart is not found, and return Decider.acceptExisting with the ItemAdded event when input is valid. Fill in the full function body.

The event model for the Stock service is approved. I need to define the StockItem entity record with fields stockId, name, reserved, and available; the StockEvent sum type with StockReserved and StockReleased constructors; and a StockId newtype wrapping UUID. Generate the Core.hs domain types file under src/Stock/Core.hs.

Write the hurl e2e test for the POST /cart/add-item endpoint. It should first create a cart with POST /cart/create, then add an item and assert the 200 response body contains itemCount 1. Also add a negative case that sends quantity -1 and expects a 422 rejection. Put both in tests/cart-add-item.hurl.

write a feature test scenario for the full checkout flow — it should drive the CreateCart → AddItem → PlaceOrder sequence through the real service handlers and assert the final CartSummary query reflects an empty cart with status Placed. I want this as a Haskell feature spec that exercises the whole slice end to end, not individual unit assertions.

Review my tests/Decider/PlaceOrderSpec.hs for NeoHaskell correctness. I'm worried I used the wrong argument order for shouldBe, that I have multiple assertions in one it block, and that I accidentally wrote spec :: IO () instead of Spec Unit. Can you check the file against project conventions and flag any anti-patterns?

Wire the RemoveItem command into the service router. I already have the Decider implemented at src/Library/Commands/RemoveItem.hs, the Projection at src/Library/Queries/LibraryCart.hs, and the Outbound at src/Library/Integrations/NotifyOnItemRemoved.hs. Now register the command handler and connect the event listeners so the feature is live.

The ItemAdded event was approved in the event model review. Now implement the event handler that appends the new item to CartEntity.items using Array.push and bumps the entity version. Generate both the event constructor in CartEvent and the update branch in the entity handler function. Entity lives in src/Cart/Core.hs.

Implement the NotifyWarehouseOnStockReserved outbound integration handler. It should pattern-match on the StockReserved event, call the warehouse HTTP endpoint to log the reservation using the Integration action DSL, and return Integration.none for all other events. The handler goes in src/Stock/Integrations/NotifyWarehouseOnStockReserved.hs.
